title: Introduction to Data Anonymisation
author: van der Burgt, Henry
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: This is a presentation on how to begin the anonymisation process in your research project. The presentation starts with a look at anonymisation in the broader chain of security strategies, including data minimisation, informed consent and access levels. Next, the presentation goes into anonymisation theory, teaching about the questions you can ask yourself when you are planning your anonymisation strategy. Lastly, the workshop will introduce some of the simpler and more accessible anonymisation techniques that can be applied to both quantitative and qualitative data. While not offering comprehensive instructions for these techniques, this will give you different approaches by which you can reduce the identifiability of your research data. Topics include: Why and when should you consider anonymisation? How does anonymisation fit into the overall security strategy of your research project? What is the level of anonymity that you should strive for? How to start planning your anonymisation strategy? What are the steps you can take to make anonymisation easier? Which basic steps and techniques can reduce the identifiability of research data?
